description: Initial setup wizard, Claude/Ollama LLM support, and local backup provider

userStories.title: Load API keys from .env files on CLI startup
userStories.description: As a user, I want billfox to automatically load API keys from ~/.billfox/.env so that I can manage my keys in one place without exporting shell variables.
acceptanceCriteria: python-dotenv is added as an explicit dependency in the cli extra in pyproject.toml
On CLI startup, billfox loads ~/.billfox/.env (global) then ./.env (project-local)
Existing env vars take precedence over .env values (standard dotenv override=False behavior)
.env is added to .gitignore
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Foundation story. Load dotenv early in cli/app.py before any command runs. Use dotenv.load_dotenv() with the two paths.

userStories.title: Add anthropic as optional dependency extra
userStories.description: As a developer, I want anthropic listed as an optional dependency so that pip install billfox[anthropic] installs everything needed for Claude.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml has new optional extra: anthropic = ["anthropic>=0.40"]
anthropic is included in the all extras group
LLMParser works with model string anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (verify in a test)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Small change. Only pyproject.toml and a verification test. pydantic-ai already routes anthropic: models.

userStories.title: Create LocalBackup provider with date-based folders
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a LocalBackup class that implements the DocumentBackup protocol so that documents can be backed up to a local folder.
acceptanceCriteria: LocalBackup class exists in src/billfox/backup/local.py implementing DocumentBackup protocol
Constructor takes base_path: str parameter
Creates date-based folder structure: {base_path}/YYYY/MM/DD/
Copies document bytes to the date folder using the original filename from source_uri
Returns BackupResult with uri pointing to the saved file path and provider='local'
If file with same name exists in target folder, overwrites it
LocalBackup is re-exported from backup/__init__.py
Typecheck passes
Tests cover: folder creation, file writing, date structure, overwrite behavior, protocol conformance
userStories.notes: Follow the same async pattern as GoogleDriveBackup: sync method wrapped in asyncio.to_thread(). Reference backup/google_drive/client.py for the date folder pattern (YYYY/MM/DD with zero-padded month/day using UTC).

userStories.title: Add Ollama model prefix support to LLMParser
userStories.description: As a developer, I want LLMParser to handle ollama:model-name strings so that Ollama models work through pydantic-ai's OpenAI-compatible provider.
acceptanceCriteria: LLMParser.__init__ accepts optional base_url: str | None = None parameter
When model starts with ollama:, _get_agent() constructs pydantic-ai OpenAIModel with OpenAIProvider(base_url='{base_url}/v1/')
Model names with tags work correctly (e.g. ollama:llama3.2:7b splits on first colon only to get model name llama3.2:7b)
Default base URL is http://localhost:11434 when base_url is None and model is ollama:*
Non-ollama models continue to work unchanged (pass model string directly to Agent)
Typecheck passes
Tests cover: ollama prefix parsing, tag handling, default base_url, non-ollama passthrough
userStories.notes: Modify src/billfox/parse/llm.py. Use lazy import: from pydantic_ai.models.openai import OpenAIModel and from pydantic_ai.providers.openai import OpenAIProvider. Verify these import paths exist in pydantic-ai>=0.1.

userStories.title: Wire backup provider selection into CLI backup command
userStories.description: As a user, I want billfox backup to use my configured backup provider (local or Google Drive) so that it works based on my config.
acceptanceCriteria: backup command reads defaults.backup.provider from config.toml
If provider is local, constructs LocalBackup with defaults.backup.local_path
If provider is google_drive, constructs GoogleDriveBackup (existing behavior)
If provider config is missing, falls back to existing GoogleDriveBackup behavior
parse command with --store also uses the configured backup provider
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Modify src/billfox/cli/backup.py to read config and build the appropriate backup instance. Also update the parse command in cli/app.py if it constructs a backup.

userStories.title: Wire Ollama config into CLI parse command
userStories.description: As a user, I want billfox parse to use my configured Ollama settings so that I don't have to pass --model and base URL every time.
acceptanceCriteria: parse command reads defaults.llm.provider, defaults.llm.model, and defaults.ollama.base_url from config
When config LLM provider is ollama, model string is constructed as ollama:{defaults.ollama.model}
base_url from config is passed to LLMParser constructor
User can override with --model ollama:other-model on the command line
When --model is not passed and config has a defaults.llm.model, that model is used as default
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Modify src/billfox/cli/app.py parse command. Read config with _read_config(), extract nested values. Pass base_url to LLMParser when model starts with ollama:.

userStories.title: Create billfox init setup wizard
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to run billfox init to interactively set up my OCR, LLM, and backup preferences so that I can start using billfox immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: billfox init command exists as a top-level CLI command in src/billfox/cli/init.py
Wizard prompts for OCR provider with numbered choices: 1=Docling (local, free), 2=Mistral (API)
Wizard prompts for LLM provider: 1=OpenAI, 2=Claude (Anthropic), 3=Ollama (local)
If Ollama selected, prompts for base URL with default http://localhost:11434
Wizard prompts for backup provider: 1=Local folder, 2=Google Drive
If Local folder selected, prompts for folder path and creates directory if needed
If Google Drive selected, tells user to run billfox auth google-drive
After selections, displays required env vars based on choices and prints a ~/.billfox/.env template
Ollama selection shows no API key guidance (local, no keys needed)

Selections saved to ~/.billfox/config.toml using nested tables: defaults.ocr.provider, defaults.llm.provider, defaults.llm.model, defaults.ollama.base_url, defaults.ollama.model, defaults.backup.provider, defaults.backup.local_path
Running billfox init again with existing config asks for confirmation before overwriting
init command is registered in cli/app.py
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create src/billfox/cli/init.py. Use typer.prompt() for input and Rich console for formatted output. Register the command in cli/app.py. LLM model strings: openai=openai:gpt-4.1, claude=anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514, ollama=ollama:{user_chosen_model}. The .env template should show lines like OPENAI_API_KEY=your-key-here only for the relevant providers.

userStories.title: Add Ollama connectivity check to init wizard
userStories.description: As a user choosing Ollama, I want the wizard to verify my Ollama server is running and show available models so I can pick the right one.
acceptanceCriteria: After entering Ollama base URL, wizard makes HTTP GET to {base_url}/api/tags
If connected, wizard displays list of available models with their names
User can select a model from the list by number
If connection fails, wizard prints warning but still saves config with a default model placeholder
Uses httpx for the HTTP request with a 5-second timeout
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Modify src/billfox/cli/init.py. httpx is already a transitive dependency. Use httpx.get() synchronously (no need for async in the CLI wizard). Parse response JSON: models are in response.json()['models'], each has a 'name' field.

userStories.title: Add config completeness guard to CLI commands
userStories.description: As a user who hasn't run setup, I want billfox to tell me to run billfox init when I try commands that need configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: extract, parse, and backup commands check for config completeness before executing
If config.toml is missing or defaults.ocr.provider is not set, prints: billfox is not configured yet. Run 'billfox init' to set up.
Guard only triggers when user does NOT pass explicit CLI flags (--extractor, --model override the need for config)
Exit with code 1 when config is missing and no CLI overrides provided
billfox init and billfox config commands are NOT guarded (always work)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Add a helper function _ensure_configured() in cli/app.py. Call it at the start of extract, parse, and backup commands. Check for the presence of defaults.ocr.provider as the minimum config indicator.

userStories.title: Tests for init command and integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive tests for the billfox init wizard so that all paths are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests use CliRunner with input= to simulate user prompts
Test: selecting Docling + OpenAI + Local backup writes correct config.toml with all nested keys
Test: selecting Mistral + Claude + Google Drive writes correct config.toml
Test: selecting Ollama triggers base URL prompt and stores ollama config
Test: re-running init with existing config asks for confirmation
Test: config guard triggers on extract/parse/backup when config is missing
Test: config guard does NOT trigger when explicit --extractor or --model flags are passed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Create tests/test_cli_init.py. Use tmp_path fixture to isolate config directory. Monkeypatch Path.home() or _get_config_dir() to use tmp_path. For Ollama connectivity test, mock httpx.get.
